root_cause: envFrom source 'payments-config' contains keys that are not valid environment variable names
evidence: ConfigMap 'payments-config' exposed invalid envFrom key(s): 1badkey, db-host, spring.profiles.active
Observed 2 InvalidEnvironmentVariableNames warning occurrence(s) for the current Pod
Container startup continued after the warning, which matches kubelet skipping invalid envFrom keys instead of blocking the Pod

causes.message: Pod imports environment variables from ConfigMap 'payments-config' using envFrom
ConfigMap 'payments-config' contains key names that cannot become environment variables
Kubelet skips invalid envFrom keys instead of injecting them into the container environment
The workload may start without configuration values it expected from envFrom

configmap:payments-config: Kubelet skipped invalid envFrom key(s): 1badkey, db-host, spring.profiles.active
Current source still contains skipped key(s): 1badkey, db-host, spring.profiles.active

likely_causes: ConfigMap or Secret keys contain dots, hyphens, or leading digits that are valid data keys but invalid environment variable names
A file-oriented configuration object was reused with envFrom without renaming the keys for environment-variable consumption
An envFrom prefix was omitted or was not enough to turn the imported keys into valid environment variable names
suggested_checks: Rename the offending keys or replace envFrom with explicit env.valueFrom entries for the keys the container actually needs
